name: Cheddar Cheese and Cauliflower Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 medium Carrots, cut into small dice
3 medium Celery stalks, cut into small dice
1 1/2 cups Chicken stock or canned chicken broth
1/2 small Cauliflower, trimmed and cut into florets (about 2 cups)
8 ounces Mild cheddar cheese, grated (plus 2 ounces for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the butter in a large saucepan.
2. Add the carrots, celery, and onion; sauté for about 5 minutes until the vegetables are softened.
3. Add the flour and cook over low heat until the flour is incorporated into the butter, about 1 minute.
4. Gradually add stock and then the cauliflower. Bring to a boil and simmer until the cauliflower is tender, or about 5 minutes.
5. Stir in the grated cheese and cook for about 30 seconds until melted (do not bring soup to a boil after cheese is added).
6. Stir in the half-and-half; warm the soup over low heat.
7. Season with cayenne pepper and salt to taste.
8. To serve, ladle the soup into warm bowls. Sprinkle each portion with the diced cheese and serve immediately.
Makes 4 servings.
